const tmpPath = '/tmp/gh-aw/aw_info.json';
fs.writeFileSync(tmpPath, JSON.stringify(awInfo, null, 2));
console.log('Generated aw_info.json at:', tmpPath);

core.setOutput('model', awInfo.model);

2026-03-09T09:23:50.9812182Z ## Fixes Applied
2026-03-09T09:23:50.9812397Z 
2026-03-09T09:23:50.9813071Z ### `Quickstart-on-how-to-write-tests.md` — Outdated mocha/chai patterns
2026-03-09T09:23:52.7058737Z The code examples still used `mocha` and `chai` APIs, but the repository migrated to **vitest**:
2026-03-09T09:23:52.7061577Z - `import { assert } from "chai"` → `import { assert, describe, it, beforeEach, afterEach } from "vitest"`  
2026-03-09T09:23:52.7065095Z - `import { Context } from "mocha"` → `import type { TestInfo } from "@azure-tools/test-recorder"`
2026-03-09T09:23:53.2070576Z - `new Recorder(context.currentTest)` → `new Recorder(context)` (vitest passes `TestInfo` directly)
2026-03-09T09:23:53.2080296Z - `beforeEach(async function (this: Context)` → `beforeEach(async (ctx) =>` (vitest arrow function pattern)
2026-03-09T09:23:54.2715911Z - Added `.js` extensions to local imports (ESM requirement)
2026-03-09T09:23:54.2719223Z - Used `import type` for type-only imports
2026-03-09T09:23:54.2719630Z 
2026-03-09T09:23:54.2725582Z ### `Generate-code-from-TypeSpec.md` — Wrong pnpm package name
2026-03-09T09:23:54.2732565Z - `npm install -g @pnpm` → `npm install -g pnpm` (`@pnpm` does not exist on npm)
2026-03-09T09:23:54.2733512Z 
2026-03-09T09:23:54.2737536Z ### `resolve-pnpm-lock-merge-conflict.md` — Broken shell command
2026-03-09T09:23:55.4763985Z - Fixed missing `/` in `` git add `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`pnpm-lock.yaml `` → `` git add `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`/pnpm-lock.yaml ``
2026-03-09T09:23:55.4765115Z 
2026-03-09T09:23:55.4765566Z ### `steps-after-generations.md` — Missing `...` and broken anchor
2026-03-09T09:23:55.4771153Z - Added missing `...` to three `pnpm build --filter=${PACKAGE_NAME}` commands (needed so Turborepo also builds dependencies)
2026-03-09T09:23:55.5966890Z - Fixed broken anchor link `#how-to-write-test-for-dpgrlc` → `#how-to-write-test-for-dpgrlcmpg` (the heading is "How to write test for DPG/RLC/MPG")

`import { assert } from \"chai\"` → `import { assert, describe, it, beforeEach, afterEach } from \"vitest\"`\n  - `import { Recorder } from \"@azure-tools/test-recorder\"` → `import type { Recorder } from \"@azure-tools/test-recorder\"` (type-only import)\n  - `import { PurviewDataMapClient } from \"../../src\"` → `import type { PurviewDataMapClient } from \"../../src/index.js\"` (ESM `.js` extension required)\n  - `import { createClient, createRecorder } from \"./utils/recordedClient\"` → add `.js` extension\n  - `beforeEach(async function () { recorder = await createRecorder(this) }` → arrow function with `ctx` parameter (vitest pattern)\n\n- **`utils/recordedClient.ts` example**:\n  - `import { Context } from \"mocha\"` → `import type { TestInfo } from \"@azure-tools/test-recorder\"` (mocha is no longer the test runner)\n  - `import { Recorder, RecorderStartOptions } from \"@azure-tools/test-recorder\"` → split into `import type` for `RecorderStartOptions`/`TestInfo` and a value import for `Recorder`\n  - `import PurviewDataMap, { PurviewDataMapClient } from \"../../../src\"` → use `.js` extension + `import type` for the type\n  - `import { ClientOptions } from \"@azure-rest/core-client\"` → `import type { ClientOptions }`\n  - `createRecorder(context: Context)` → `createRecorder(context: TestInfo)`\n  - `new Recorder(context.currentTest)` → `new Recorder(context)` (the `TestInfo` object is passed directly, not `.currentTest`)\n\n- **`monitor.spec.ts` example** (HLC pattern):\n  - `import { assert } from \"chai\"` → `import { assert, describe, it, beforeEach, afterEach } from \"vitest\"`\n  - Removed `import { Context } from \"mocha\"` (no longer needed)\n  - Split `RecorderStartOptions` into `import type`\n  - `import { MonitorClient } from \"../src/monitorClient\"` → add `.js` extension\n  - `beforeEach(async function (this: Context) { recorder = new Recorder(this.currentTest) }` → arrow function with `ctx` using vitest pattern\n\nThese changes match the actual patterns in `sdk/purview/purview-datamap-rest/test/public/` and `sdk/monitor/arm-monitor/test/monitor.spec.ts`.\n\n### `documentation/Generate-code-from-TypeSpec.md`\n\n- `npm install -g @pnpm` → `npm install -g pnpm`\n\n  The package `@pnpm` does not exist on npm. The correct package name is `pnpm`.\n\n### `documentation/resolve-pnpm-lock-merge-conflict.md`\n\n- Fixed missing `/` path separator in the combined one-liner command:\n  ````\n  # Before (broken):\n  git add `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`pnpm-lock.yaml\n  # After (correct):\n  git add `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`/pnpm-lock.yaml\n  ````\n\n### `documentation/steps-after-generations.md`\n\n- Added missing `...` suffix to three `pnpm build --filter=${PACKAGE_NAME}` commands (Windows record, Linux playback, Windows playback). Without `...`, Turborepo does not build the package's dependencies, which can cause build failures. The Linux record command already had `...` correctly.\n\n- Fixed broken anchor link `#how-to-write-test-for-dpgrlc` → `#how-to-write-test-for-dpgrlcmpg`. The target heading is `# How to write test for DPG/RLC/MPG`, which produces the anchor `#how-to-write-test-for-dpgrlcmpg`; the old link was missing `mpg`.\n","branch":"docs/fix-documentation-inconsistencies","title":"Fix documentation inconsistencies in documentation/ folder"},"name":"create_pull_request"}}}
